commit.message:
fix: remove dying /bin/true procd instance that breaks UCI reload triggers (#30)

* fix: remove dummy /bin/true procd instance that causes service to die and breaks UCI triggers

The start_service() in /etc/init.d/tailscale-settings created a procd instance
running /bin/true with respawn 3600 1 3. Since /bin/true exits in milliseconds
(way below the 3600s threshold), every exit counted as a crash. After 3 crashes,
procd marks the service as dead and may stop processing its UCI config-change
triggers (registered via procd_add_reload_trigger), meaning LuCI Save & Apply
never called reload_service() → apply_settings() → tailscale set.

Fix: remove the procd instance. tailscale-settings is a config-only service.
The trigger registration happens in _procd_close_service() via service_triggers()
regardless of whether any procd instances exist.

Co-authored-by: Tokisaki-Galaxy <61621971+Tokisaki-Galaxy@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Security: Fix command injection via LLM output in summary workflow (#167)

* Initial plan

* 🔒 security(workflow): fix command injection in summary.yml

Replace direct LLM output interpolation in shell command with the
already-defined RESPONSE env variable. Previously,
`${{ steps.inference.outputs.response }}` was single-quote-delimited
inside a `run` block, allowing a crafted LLM response containing a
single quote to escape the quoting context and inject arbitrary shell
commands (potentially leaking GITHUB_TOKEN).

The RESPONSE env var was already present in the `env` block but unused.
This change references it as `"$RESPONSE"` and also double-quotes
`"$ISSUE_NUMBER"` for consistency.

Fixes: command injection per security report.

VPLAY-12854 getfile to use std vector follow-up (#1130)

VPLAY-12854 Change GetFile to use std::vector instead of AampGrowableBuffer

Reason for Change: Refactors AAMP’s download buffer plumbing to use std::vector<uint8_t> end-to-end (notably PrivateInstanceAAMP::GetFile and CurlCallbackContext), aligning core download paths, cache handling, and unit tests with the newer growable-buffer maturation work.

Test Guidance: this is pure tech debt - need do-no-harm general regression testing and confirmation that no impact on existing L1/L2/L3 tests

Risk: Medium (many changes - should be safe/compiler guided, but big change set nevertheless)

fix: harden against crash vectors found by chaos testing

Added 12 chaos tests exercising crash vectors, and fixed 6 production
code issues they uncovered:

Fixes:
- NaN/Inf guard in estimate_text_height: non-finite or non-positive
  font_size/wrap_width now fall back to sane defaults instead of
  propagating NaN through height calculations and viewport culling.
- Span bounds clamping in build_layout_job and render_text_with_links:
  span.start/end are now clamped to text.len(), preventing index-OOB
  panics from malformed spans.
- u32 saturation in StyledText::push_text: text offsets and char_count
  now saturate at u32::MAX instead of silently wrapping on >4GB strings.
- Render depth limit (MAX_RENDER_DEPTH=128): render_blocks bails at
  depth 128 to prevent stack overflow from pathologically nested
  blockquotes/lists.
- Non-finite scroll offset guard in show_scrollable: NaN/Inf scroll_y
  values are ignored instead of being passed to egui.

Chaos tests cover: deep nesting (500 levels), NaN/Inf float inputs,
empty tables with 0 columns, unclosed code fences, large list inputs,
u32 saturation, and out-of-bounds span offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix thread-safety bugs in PaperTradingService and DynamicConfigurationService

- PaperTradingService.UpdateMetrics: Wrap all metric writes inside
  lock(_accountLock) to prevent torn reads when other threads read
  metrics concurrently. Sub-method calls (CalculateMaxDrawdown,
  CalculateStreaks, CalculateBestPerformers, UpdateInstrumentMetrics)
  are also covered by the lock scope.

- DynamicConfigurationService.ApplyOptimizedParametersAsync: Wrap
  early-validation profile mutations (daily limit reset, cooldown
  metrics, degradation counter) inside _writeLock to prevent races
  with LearnFromTradeAsync. Also protect the no-changes else branch.

commit.message:
debug: disable FW version stamp — suspected infinite reboot loop

The FW stamp code calls bdb_resetLocalAction() which triggers SystemReset().
If NV item 0x0408 is being erased during the reset (by ZCD_STARTOPT_DEFAULT_NETWORK_STATE
processing), the stamp never persists and the device reboots forever.

Temporarily disabled to restore basic functionality.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Auto-QA checks: color contrast, bundle analysis, large file detection

self-improvement analysis issue:

commit.message:
fix: enable timer monitor for reliable daily cache refresh on Flex Consumption

use_monitor=False disabled the singleton blob lease that the Azure Functions
runtime uses to track schedule state. On Flex Consumption (scales to zero),
without this lease the runtime has no persistent record of the timer schedule
and cannot reliably wake the app to fire the trigger — resulting in the daily
refresh_sku_cache never executing.

Setting use_monitor=True restores the blob lease, ensuring the timer fires
reliably even after cold starts or app restarts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
